prediction.reasoning:
The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, which assumes log returns are normally distributed. Historical daily adjusted close prices for SPGI over the past 500 trading days (~2 years) were used to estimate the daily drift (mean of log returns) and daily volatility (standard deviation of log returns). For the 18-day prediction horizon, the expected future price (point estimate) is calculated using the estimated drift. Confidence intervals are derived from the distribution of the log-price at time t, accounting for both the drift and volatility over the prediction period. Specifically, ln(S_t/S_0) is assumed to be normally distributed with mean (μ - σ^2/2)t and standard deviation σ√t.
Calculated daily drift (μ): 0.000260, Daily volatility (σ): 0.016335
